segment-text: (Based on the novel, by Joe Dunthorne)
London Office 11 Francis Street, London, SW1P 1DE
Tel. + 44 207 306 5155 / info@protagonistpictures.com
INT. OLIVER'S BEDROOM- DAY
OLIVER lying in bed. He gets up, looks in the mirror, takes down a dictionary from his shelf, looks up a word, ticks it off, puts on a record and opens the curtains.

Most people think of themselves as individuals. That there is no-one on the planet like them. This thought motivates them to get out of bed and eat and walk around like nothing's wrong. My name is Oliver
We cut outside and slowly zoom back to show the town in which he lives. A caption: A Winter in Wales, a little while ago'. Note we also do this in static, non-zoomed shots.
EXT. VARIOUS- DAY
STILL SHOTS throughout the town in which OLIVER TATE lives. Titles. We end on a shot of a school.
INT. SCHOOL CLASS - MORNING

We track down a school corridor, ending up at a classroom. MR DAVEY, AN ENGLISH TEACHER, addresses the class (14/15). The camera follows a note passed between the pupils, ABBY SMUTS; CHIPS (cocky, smirking); MARK PRITCHARD and finally to JORDANA BEVAN (bored, casually flaming the underside of her desk with a lighter); OLIVER TATE watches her.

What kind of young person am I? That's the challenge. We talk about challenges, don't we? Well that's the challenge I'm giving you this term. A gauntlet, so to speak. But this gauntlet is also an opportunity. An invitation, if you will, to self-discovery. What do I mean by self-discovery. Mark
Pritchard..?
Having a wank?
MR.DAVEY CLICKS HIS FINGERS AND SENDS HIM OUT.
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 1A.
Self-discovery. Who am I? What a question - let's look at that...etc
I find that the only way to get through life is to picture myself in an entirely disconnected reality - I often imagine how people would react to my death:
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 2. INT. SCHOOL CORRIDOR/QUAD
Track in to tanoy.
Mr. Dunthorne's quavering voice as he makes the announcement MR DUNTHORNE (O.S.) He was a gifted pupil, loved by all. It's not only a loss to the school, it's a loss to the Welsh nation.
INT/EXT. SCHOOL VARIOUS
We see what OLIVER describes.
- the shocked faces of my

-- THE GYM. SOME GIRLS STOP A NETBALL GAME. -- FOOTBALL FIELD. BOYS STAND AROUND IN GAMES KIT, BUT WITH F BLACK AMRM BANDS. -- CLASSROOM - WE TRACK INTO THE FACES OF SOME KIDS. -- SWIMMING POOL, EMPTY. A YOUNG CHILD IN SWIMMING COSTUME G BEING COMFORTED BY HIS PE TEACHER, AN ATTRACTIVE WOMAN. a playground bedecked with flowers - -- FLOWERS/TRIBUTES/PICTURES BY THE SCHOOL GATES; PLAYGROUND; QUAD, BY A BENCH. the empty stillness of a school corridor - a young child trying to make sense of it all Shot of a small child being comforted by a teacher. A REPORTER talks to a news camera in the playground. Floral tributes behind her. We would need a prop camera and a cameraman.
OLIVER (V.O.)
local news analysis WE see early school pictures of OLIVER, full screen, smiling and full of life.. We see a newspaper with the caption 'OLIVER TATE DEAD'
So I suppose the question is, what now?
tear-streaked tributes News footage of school children crying -
VIDEO 8
Plate shot of TV to comp in footage.
The studied stoicism of my parents.

We have a shot of LLOYD and JILL at a table with a plain background. Camera bulbs flash off - they are making a statement for camera. WE SHOW THIS ON A TV. NOTE - IS IT POSSIBLE TO HAVE SOME INSERTS OF FLASH BULBS/REPORTER CAMERAS.
EXT. SCHOOL/GATES NIGHT
candle-lit vigils
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 3.
Note: WE SEE A BEAUTIFUL WOMAN, CRYING AND CARRYING A PICTURE OF OLIVER. SHOTS OF CUDDLY TOYS/PHOTOS, A DRAWING OF OLIVER WITH THE INSCRIPTION, IN ITALICS: 'OLIVER TATE R.I.P. WE ENVY THE ANGELS'
- and, finally, my glorious resurrection
EXT. SCHOOL - DAY

Six or so girls, including Abby and Jordana are draped over one another by a tree, crying. Blinking through the tears, they see something. It's OLIVER. He's slightly glowing, like Obi-Wan in STAR WARS. He has a serene look to him. The GIRLS look perplexed.
Don't ask how. Just know that I am now more powerful than ever.
The girls look back, in awe.
Let's get off.

OLIVER allows himself a small smile. He is staring at the back of JORDANA'S NECK. She looks round. OLIVER awkwardly looks away. MR. DAVEY is wrapping things up. MARK PRITCHARD passes OLIVER a note. He indicates that it's from JORDANA. OLIVER opens it. It says, 'If you've opened this note, you're a cock-riding twat'. He tries to pass it to ZOE (15, OVERWEIGHT/STUDIOUS). She refuses it.

Play the game, get stuck in, and let's make this term one to remember. OK. Let's get down to business. Page 23 in your blue books. Oliver Tate you can come up and read out that note as it seems so interesting.
OLIVER walks up. The class woofs.
What does it say?
The bell goes. The class breaks up. MR DAVEY does some crowd control.
Alright etc you've been saved this time etc.
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 3A.
Oliver looks at JORDANA slope out. MARK PRITCHARD puts his arm around ABBY.
INT. SCHOOL CORRIDOR - MORNING
Establishers of children in the corridors. OLIVER is looking at JORDANA. SLOW MOTION (in OLIVER'S head) she turns back and looks at OLIVER, lovingly. We move into OLIVER.
INT. SCHOOL CORRIDOR - MORNING
OLIVER walks out of class, the corridor is nearly empty.
EXT. SCHOOL GROUNDS.
OLIVER spying on JORDANA. By the trees or bike sheds. JORDANA is sitting by herself burning things or smoking.
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 4. INT. SCHOOL REFECTORIES - LUNCH
OLIVER'S QUEUING for lunch. CHIPS and KEIRON enters the frame. We establish GENE the dinner lady here. You know Jordana Bevan's back on
You pushed! The word on the playground is - Prichard fingered
Abby Smuts at the Blue Light Disco. Bevan found them in the toilets with Pritchard up to his knuckles. We see ABBY with PRITCHARD across the refectory.
Thought you'd be interested. So you won't mind if I have a go?
No - it's fine. We're just friends...etc
OLIVER SHRUGS. JORDANA comes OVER
Hey, Jordana.
Do you want a picture?
Smooth, Ol.
Wasn't trying to be - so you haven't got me.
EXT. SCHOOL - LUNCH
OLIVER has found himself a secluded place. He watching CHIPS and KEIRON, start to tease ZOE, an OVERWEIGHT GIRL (15). But, mainly, he sees how JORDANA is laughing and delighting in the teasing.
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 4A.
Lately, I've become interested in the theory of cognitive dissonance.
We see an appropriate encyclopedia entry.
We TRACK into a TV - on it is a professor on an O.U.-TYPE TV show, he has an accent of some kind. Perhaps German
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 5.

- contradictory thoughts or beliefs serve as a driving force that compel the human mind to acquire or invent new thoughts or beliefs so as to minimise the amount of dissonance between such cognitions or thoughts.
We CUT TO a chart drawn by OLIVER. STICK MEN.
It's all about balance. While I essentially disapprove of bullying, I'm also cognisant of the value of appearing to fit in. And, further, my need to socially advance myself in schoolC
We see this on a NEW CHART. A STICK FIGURE of OLIVER separated from a group by a hurdle titled 'WILLINGNESS TO BULLY'.
- conflicts with being able to show basic humanity to others. So I must invent a new thought. Perhaps Zoe's so fat that she doesn't have real feelings?
This logic is backed up by further line drawings. We CUT to: CHIPS running off with ZOE'S BAG. WE SEE OLIVER DECIDE TO FOLLOW.
EXT. WOODS NEAR SCHOOL - LATE AFTERNOON

OLIVER is running with ZOE'S BAG. He lobs it over to JORDANA, who throws it to CHIPS. CHIPS does some good verbal taunts and ducks and dives. Note: we also do a version where we start with Chips. ZOE follows, lamely protesting.
Stop it. Please!
Chips is an outstanding bully. He once made our religious education teacher cry using only blu tack.
INT. CLASSROOM - DAY

The RELIGIOUS EDUCATION TEACHER, MISS DUTTON, walks in. She has a distinctive mole on her chin. She looks up at the class. Everyone has blu tac moles on their chins. She starts to cry and runs out of the room.
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 6.
What did I tell you? I fucking told you.
EXT. WOODS - CONTINUOUS
ZOE gathers her belongings up and clutches them to her chest.
That's it, Fat, almost there.
They reach a small stagnant pond. They form a circle round and chuck the bag between them over the pond.
Give it back [...]

She manages to grab the bag from OLIVER, there's a scuffle, a shove, and she starts to fall. The image freezes as ZOE teeters on one leg, her hand outstretched towards the pencil case. We see close, frozen shots of everyone.
I don't know which one of us put a hand to Zoe's back. For a moment I imagine that she might just balance there, a fat ballerina on one leg, but her
foot slips from under her and she belly-flops into the shallow
gloop.
The image unfreezes and we see Zoe pushed into the shallow gloop. Her ruler, pens and pencils float on the thick algae. She writhes around and we close in on OLIVER'S FACE.

We all feel proud; as Zoe begins to sob, shirt splattered green, stationery slowly sinking, we know that this would be one of those vivid memories from youth that Mr Davey told us about in this morning's lesson.
The kids run away. OLIVER stays there frozen. He looks between the fleeing bullies and the victim, eyes wide, his face scrolling through various emotions.
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 7. EXT. SCHOOL POND - LATER
OLIVER helps ZOE out of the pond. SHE SHOVES him away with her MUDDY HANDS.
EXT. WOODS - MOMENTS LATER
OLIVER and ZOE walk along, far apart from one another. OLIVER is now also covered in mud. They've clearly fought.
I was trying to stop you falling.
Leave me alone please. Then go fuck yourself.
INT./EXT. VARIOUS OVER MUSIC: OLIVER WALKING HOME, LONELY 15
In many ways I prefer my own company. It gives me time to think.
We see OLIVER sitting in an unusual place, perhaps a tree, A reading. Also - in a bath tub by the industrial estate. Walking along, bouncing a tennis ball.
I suppose it's a bit of an affectation, but I often read the dictionary. My word for today is flaigitious, which means 'wickedly shameful'..
We see an insert.
and stare at the sea as though
OLIVER STANDS ON THE WALL BY BARRY BEACH, LOOKING OUT. He walks to the beach. He looks up at the sky.
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 7A.
But recently I've been getting the feeling that I'm not quite here, as though there's another Oliver Tate inside me, operating
the levers of a life-sized Olivershaped shell. I've just turned
face of a forty year old.
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 8.
He looks back to the sea and watches the waves hitting his outstretched feet. He doesn't move, and allows the water to lap around him.
EXT. STREET OUTSIDE OLIVER'S HOUSE- EVENING
OLIVER sees GRAHAM'S van.
EXT. OLIVER'S HOUSE - EVENING
OLIVER, SODDEN FEET, COVERED IN MUD, approaches his front door, takes a deep breath and walks in.
Oliver? What happened to you? We were waiting for you.
INT. OLIVER'S HOUSE - EVENING
OLIVER is in the KITCHEN as his MOTHER, JILL (40), tries to get the mud off his SCHOOL UNIFORM with a stiff brush.
My mother is worried that I have mental problems. I found a book about teenage paranoid delusions during a routine search of my parents' bedroom.
We see a cutaway of OLIVER making this discovery.
After that I started slipping certain choice phrases into our conversations.
INT. OLIVER'S BEDROOM - EVENING
We jump cut through OLIVER'S DIFFERENT responses. OLIVER tucked in , his mother perched on the end of the bed
My body has been replaced by a shell/ My internal organs are made of stone/ I have been dead for years.
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 9. INT. OLIVER'S HOUSE - EVENING
A dinner party is in progress. OLIVER'S parents, LLOYD and JILL along with JILL'S WORK COLLEAGUE, JACKIE and her husband MALCOLM. JILL and LLOYD clear the table
It's perfectly normal to have an active imagination at Oliver's age.
Thank you, Malcolm.
(to LLOYD) That trout was glorious by the way.
But where's he got it from? Not from Lloyd. He doesn't have an imaginative bone in his body.
Marine biology is pretty creative
You research and categorise fish,
That's one part of it - you know I was on Open University once OLIVER Shall I show them the tape, Dad?
Stay at the table, please Ol. I said to Lloyd should we send him to a psychiatrist? But he said we couldn't afford it.
I said it might not be worth the money at this point.
Cos Lloyd can get a little bit like that. Shut off. You know, like he's not there
The invisible man!
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 10.
Good humoured dinner party laughter.
INT. OFFICE - DAY
JILL is in a boring meeting.

My mum's lucky because her mental health problems can be mistaken for charm and sociability. Perhaps her job's to blame. She works for the council's legal services department. One of the rules in her office is that, if it is your birthday, you are held responsible for bringing your own cake to work. We see a brief insert of JILL guiltily producing a cake at work.
EXT. SEA - LLOYD IN A ROW BOAT/INT. MUSEUM
My Dad divides his time between the museum and isolated lakes, finding fish that only he and maybe four other professors in the world would even know about. Adults are full of secrets.
INT. OLIVER'S HOUSE. HIS PARENT'S BEDROOM- DAY
We cut to a shot of LLOYD (40) lying in bed. He wears a TATTY, OLD DRESSING GOWN and is sipping from a mug.

For some time I wondered why dad would stay in bed for days at a time drinking endless amounts of hot lemon, using the same mug over and over and never washing it. But then, one day, I solved that mystery.

We cut to the BATHROOM. OLIVER sees a bottle of antidepressants. We see it's made out to LLOYD TATE. He pours out some tablets into his hand. They're BRIGHT BLUE. HE LOOKS UP THE NAME OF THE DRUG IN THE MEDICAL DICTIONARY AA AND WE SEE A DEFINITION.
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 11.
I keep the empty bottles in a box of my old toys.
We see an INSERT of this.
I've also learned that my parents have not had sex for seven months.
WE CUT to OLIVER checking the dimmer switch in his parents C bedroom. He walks back to his bedroom - he exchanges a quizzical look with JILL.
I've been monitoring their intimacy via the dimmer switch in their bedroom. I know when they've been at it because the next morning the dial will be set
to half way. He writes his findings in his book. We see a graph with 'dimmer switch position' on one axis and 'likelihood of parents' divorce' on another.
I've seen their dry, pursed kisses grow ever more bloodless.
We see a series of unaffectionate kisses between them.
INT. OLIVER'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
Peering through the crack of his door, OLIVER sees LLOYD walking down the stairs with some bedding.
INT. OLIVER'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
OLIVER watches TV on a small, portable set. A father and son are talking on a rooftop.
It's no wonder I've retreated into fantasy: I wish life could be more like American soap
operas.
You can tell me anything, you know that, Dad.
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 12.
Thanks son. You're my best buddy. It's been so tough since your mother died...
That wasn't your fault.
I should never have taken the yacht out in that storm... Sometimes I hate the sea so much.
You're going to make this restaurant work. I swear you will. You're a great chef. And a great dad.
You're such a great son. I can't believe you're twelve already. My little baby's a man. I love you so much.
SUDDENLY, TV KID TWO APPEARS.
Dad! It's Billy. He's bleeding! He's bleeding everywhere!
Then, whenever things got dramatic, you could just fade the picture down and pick things up again later.
INT. OLIVER'S BEDROOM - NIGHT

OLIVER looks out the window. He sees a house opposite that has no curtains. It is sparsely furnished. The walls are white and the furniture is either cream or covered in dust covers. Oliver can see A MAN performing various NINJA-LOOKING moves. This is GRAHAM (30s/40s). (Possibly in the garden) KIM-LIN, an attractive JAPANESE GIRL walks up behind him. She puts her hand on GRAHAM'S CHEST. They start to kiss passionately and fall to the floor, as if about to have sex. They disappear from Oliver's view. Oliver's shocked, halfrepelled, half-intrigued.
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 13. INT. OLIVER'S HOUSE - NIGHT
We see LLOYD folding out the sofa bed. LLOYD lies down on it and stares up at the ceiling.
INT. OLIVER'S HOUSE. HIS PARENT'S BEDROOM - NIGHT

JILL is emptying out a box onto the bed. She looks through various photos of herself and LLOYD. We see a picture of her when she was young, with LONG HAIR, somewhere vaguely idyllic and sunny. There is also a line drawing of her, signed 'Love, G.' She then notices OLIVER watching her, walks to the door and closes it.
Goodnight, Oliver
We FADE DOWN like a soap opera.
INT. HALL. OLIVER'S HOUSE - DAY
OLIVER is standing next to LLOYD, his FATHER. OLIVER looks at the sofa in the living room. On it sits a pillow and a neatly folded duvet. LLOYD turns towards the door.
Tell your mother to hurry up. I'll wait in the road.
OLIVER walks up the stairs and into his parents room. He hits the dimmer switch on the wall. The ceiling light comes on harshly.
You look nice.
I'm not ready.
You look good for a mum.
Yes. Well I'm younger than most mums.
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 14.
Dad says to hurry up.
EXT/INT. OLIVER'S HOUSE - MORNING
OLIVER runs downstairs
Mum's getting the bus!

He opens the front door and sees LLOYD talking to the next door neighbour, GRAHAM. GRAHAM is next to his YOUNG, BEAUTIFUL JAPANESE GIRLFRIEND KIM-LIN. LLOYD is visibly tense and keeps trying to half-get into his car. OLIVER watches surreptitiously. We focus on OLIVER'S REACTION to the following scene.
Well - terrific. Such a small world.
Yes - well it's such a great area. I love it
She just loves it.
Highest cancer rates in Europe!
Well, that's what took my uncle.
I'm so sorry. It's a lovely house.
Thank you. My uncle was gay, so I get to inherit it all. And this was Jill's grandmother's house, right... Yes.
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 15.
Jill and I would spend hours up in that room. Listening to records, hatching plans.
Right. So are you going to sell up or...
I'm gonna let things take their own path.
That's great. To have the time to do that.
I work to live, not live to work. So - I was saying to Jill we should all meet up LLOYD Yes. Absolutely. That'd be lovely. We'll consult and be in touch. Can't wait. When did you see Jill?
Just in town. I think compatible energies are destined to keep interconnecting. I agree.
Great. I'm sure you're right..
Graham gives Lloyd a long man hug.
Give my love to Jill.
Will do. Great to see you. Great we're so close, now! In proximity.
GRAHAM and KIM-LIN wander back down the road to a psychedelic VAN. GRAHAM trots back to LLoyd.
Hey - Lloyd. Sorry - did you hear me and Kim Lin having sex outside on the grass last night?
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 15A.
Well, if you do just let me know. She's a lovely girl, but she's a bit of a howler.

He walks back to the van which has has a big picture of GRAHAM on the side saying 'THE GRAHAM T. PURVIS SYSTEM: psychic and physical excellence' amongst a STARBURST of ASTROLOGICAL signs. LLOYD, looking lonely, waves after them as they drive off. OLIVER approaches.
Mum's getting the bus.
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 16.
Right. I'll give you a lift to school then.
Can I do the gears? Okay.
What did they want?
Dinner. Not on my watch though.
Why were you being so friendly, then?
You can't let people like that know what you're thinking.
What kind of people are they?
LLOYD turns to OLIVER with purpose Mystics.
OLIVER nods understandingly.
INT. CAR - MORNING
LLOYD drives.
How's school etc Any girls - there's plenty of time for that nonsense etc Look: that's the thing we saw yesterday etc
Third, please. And then into fifth. Save a bit of petrol. She's been thirsty recently.
OLIVER puts the car into third gear and then into fourth There's a pause.
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 16A.
You can say what you like about Wales but you cannot beat it for scenery.
We see OLIVER take in a beautiful vista. He looks at in the side mirror, reflected in the car.
I'm not sure I believe in scenery.
Lloyd nods. The car stops. We see OLIVER get out.
They shake hands.
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 17.
I'll pick you up at five. Go get 'em, killer.
Oliver starts walking.
As usual, Dad drops me 800 yards from school.
We see him approach his SCHOOL.
That way I can slip in unnoticed, like a torpedo.
Oliver is charged by CHIPS and KERION. He does an undignified swerve in order to avoid them.
INT. SCHOOL CLASSROOM - MORNING
MR.DAVEY is taking the register. We see that ZOE isn't there. OLIVER looks concerned.
EXT. SCHOOL PLAYGROUND
Later - OLIVER looks for ZOE, but can't find her.
INT. CAR - AFTERNOON
OLIVER travels home, unsettled. JILL sits in the back.
INT. OLIVER'S HOUSE. LIVING ROOM- EVENING
The TATES are watching television, we can't see the programme.
I used to have long hair. Like her. Hmm?
I used to have long hair. Remember?
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 17A.
Yes.
She's pretty, isn't she/
Er... I don't know
Did you like it when I had long hair? Er...
Or short like now?
I like your hair no matter how long it is.
But did you prefer it long?
Either way is good
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 18.
No opinion?
It's up to you.
So it wouldn't bother you if I shaved it off?
Not if it made you happy.
If you want to shave off your hair, you should mum. Definitely.
Why would I shave my hair of Lloyd?
She walks off.
What a stupid idea.
INT. OLIVER'S BEDROOM - EARLY MORNING
OLIVER takes out some paper from a drawer. He feeds it into a typewriter and starts to type.
Zoe Preece hasn't been in school the past few days so I've written a pamphlet giving her a few pointers on how to break out of the victim cycle.

We see close INSERTS of the following snippets: "Weirdly, you're not that fat. You're fat in the mind" "Maintain a facade of complete indifference." "Transform any facet of your personality to fit in." "You are who you pretend to be." "If you must study, do so in private." "Don't cry to the dinner ladies." Some are in italics, some are underlined, some are in capitals.
I've also given her two examples to inspire her.

We cut to RHYDIAN BIRD. He is in the playground and has pulled his pants down to fart. He is surrounded by clapping pupils chanting 'Rhydian! Rhydian! including OLIVER, CHIPS and KEIRON. As many of our featured kids as possible.'
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 19.
Firstly, Rhydian Bird. He once pulled his trousers down in the playground to fart and followed through, coiling a unhealthy turd onto the tarmac. But, instead of
looking embarrassed, he screamed
We see OLIVER'S AWED REACTION. WE cut to CHIPS.
Secondly, Chips. He wasn't even considered that hard until Keiron King famously stabbed him in the back with my compass.
We see this. He said nothing, showed no discomfort as his shirt blossomed

OLIVER gathers up several A4 pages, signs 'Good luck! Best wishes for a persecution-free future, Oliver Tate' at the bottom, and puts them in a brown manila envelope. He then searches in his drawer for something. It's a '50p off' voucher for a Burger Chain. He pops it in.
I'm not quite sure that I've found my special skill yet. I've tried smoking a pipe; flipping coins; listening exclusively to French crooners; I even had a brief hat phase, but nothing
WE SEE BRIEF INSERTS: OLIVER behind the bike sheds with a pipe; flipping a coin under a street lamp; putting on a Serge Gainsbourg record; wearing a cowboy hat at dinner with his parents.
Maybe my mission in life is to help others. (MORE)
Like the Samaritans, but a version that specifically targets the obese. I need to find out who I am.
Insert: Oliver's notebook on his desk. Who am I? Investigate. He rips out the paper and pins it to the wall. EXT. TATE CAR/SCHOOL OLIVER is driven to school. OLIVER walks into school
EXT. SCHOOL. BEHIND THE REFECTORIES - EARLY MORNING
OLIVER tentatively pads up to GENE, a dinner lady. He has put the collar up on his jacket, as if he's in a Noir film.
The most secure route to Zoe is Big Gene who, over the last few months, had become her only friend and confidant.
We see a brief shot of ZOE crying next to GENE, who's comforting her. Gene told me that Zoe was very
OLIVER produces the now dog-eared A4 envelope from his rucksack. We CUT TO JORDANA, watching the scene from afar.
That's a big love letter.
I've got a big heart.
Taxi driver gesture as he touches his heart, Betsy's desk
Sometimes I wish there was a film crew following my every move. I imagine the camera craning up as
I walk away. But, unless things
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 20A.
OLIVER does a little twirl as he walks away. We Zoom out to over JORDANA'S SHOULDER. JORDANA REACTS.
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 21. INT. SCIENCE LAB - MORNING
OLIVER is dissecting a frog. His lab partner is JORDANA. She is holding a bit of the creature while OLIVER makes incisions with a scalpel.
I feel bad cutting into its head.
Why? it's just a fucking frog.
It's still a thing though. It had life.
I wonder where they get the frogs?
I don't know. Probably a lake or river nearby. They probably have a guy who does it. I could find out. My dad's in this area.
I don't actually want to know.
Neither do I. I'm just saying I can find out. You asked.
I wonder where Fat is. Zoe?
She hasn't been in school for a while, has she?
Hasn't she? I hadn't noticed.
There's an awkward pause.
I would've thought you would. She leaves quite a big gap.
She's not really on my radar...
Do you think it's because we pushed her into the pond?
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 21A.
I didn't push her.
Do you think she's at home just weeping herself to death?
I didn't push her.
Maybe you should write to her. Maybe you should maybe write her a pamphlet or something. What do you think?
ECU SCALPEL IN FROG, PAN UP TO OLIVER
What is this? What do you want?
Meet me under the railway bridge after school and we'll take it from there. Bring a polaroid camera and a diary. Okay?
SUBMARINE 23rd NOVEMBER 2009 RED 22. EXT. UNDER THE BRIDGE - AFTERNOON
OLIVER walks, carrying his bag. Note we need a place that's different to the bridge for another stretch of walking. He is extremely uncertain. JORDANA is there waiting, smoking a cigarette.
She looked good except for a small patch of dry skin between
her index and middle fingers.
Have you got the camera and diary?
OLIVER gets his camera from his bag and shows a brand new diary.
Do you have the pamphlet?
Give me the camera.
Tentatively, OLIVER approaches. He hands her the camera.
Okay. Now, kneel down.

OLIVER does so. JORDANA leans down into a kissing position. OLIVER, TENTATIVELY, KISSES her. He closes his eyes. We hear a camera flash and the whirr of the motor. Startled, Oliver looks to the camera. Polaroids spit out onto the floor. OLIVER looks at a patch of ECZEMA on JORDANA'S NECK.
